message:
feat(ui): Comprehensive theme system with WCAG accessibility support

This commit significantly expands the theme infrastructure to support
multiple presets, accessibility validation, and professional-grade
aesthetics:

Theme Presets:
- Dark (default): Balanced blue-accent scheme optimized for low-light
- Light: High-contrast scheme for bright environments
- HighContrast: AAA-compliant scheme for maximum accessibility
- Nord: Arctic color palette for a calm, muted aesthetic

Accessibility Features:
- WCAG 2.1 contrast validation at AA, AALarge, and AAA levels
- Relative luminance calculation per W3C specification
- Contrast ratio helper functions for palette validation
- Theme selection based on accessibility requirements

API Additions:
- ThemePreset enum with from_str parsing and iteration
- ThemePalette::from_preset() for preset-based initialization
- ContrastLevel enum for accessibility tier selection
- contrast_ratio() and meets_contrast() utility functions
- highlight_style() method for search result highlighting

The highlight_style uses the theme's accent color as background,
ensuring consistent branding while maintaining readability through
automatic foreground color selection based on luminance.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -58,6 +58,20 @@ pub fn search_bar(
                 .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
             palette.border_focus_style(),
         ),
+        InputMode::PaneFilter => (
+            " Filter: Pane ".to_string(),
+            Style::default()
+                .fg(palette.accent_alt)
+                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
+            palette.border_focus_style(),
+        ),
+        InputMode::DetailFind => (
+            " Detail Find ".to_string(),
+            Style::default()
+                .fg(palette.accent_alt)
+                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
+            palette.border_focus_style(),
+        ),
     };
     let title = Span::styled(title_text, title_style);
